guidelines: Binary presence per classic foundation (virtue OR vice counts as present), MFRC-style. Author-coded starter set — small (§5 targets 200-400); expand and add coders over time.

items.text: A mother shields her toddler from the blast, absorbing the shrapnel herself.
Volunteers rushed food and blankets to families left homeless by the flood.
The company knowingly sold contaminated medicine that sickened hundreds of children.
Judges must apply the same rules to the powerful and the poor alike.
He cheated on the exam and then lied to the board about it.
The referee took a bribe to fix the championship match.
Citizens should stand by their nation in its hour of need.
He betrayed his comrades, handing their names to the enemy.
True patriots never abandon their flag, even in defeat.
Soldiers honored their fallen brothers at the memorial.
Obey the chain of command; discipline holds the unit together.
The protesters defied the mayor's curfew and marched anyway.
Respect for tradition and elders is the backbone of our community.
Students must not question the teacher's rulings in class.
The rebels overthrew the lawful government in a violent coup.
Desecrating the temple with graffiti outraged the faithful.
The monks kept the sacred texts pure for a thousand years.
Pornography and drug use are defiling our neighborhoods, the pastor warned.
Blessed are the peacemakers, for they shall be called children of God.
The ritual bath purifies the body before entering the shrine.
Quarterly earnings rose three percent on strong ad revenue.
The city council approved a new bike lane on Fifth Avenue.
Scientists mapped the genome of a deep-sea worm.
The forecast calls for rain across the Midwest this weekend.
Tech stocks tumbled as investors feared rising interest rates.
Nurses worked double shifts to comfort the dying during the outbreak.
The senator demanded equal pay for equal work across genders.
A whistleblower exposed the firm's fraud, then was fired in retaliation.
Draft dodgers were branded traitors to their country.
The general disobeyed the president's direct order.
They vandalized the war memorial, spray-painting slurs on the graves.
The charity diverted donations meant for orphans into private accounts.
Families gathered to celebrate the harvest festival together.
He gave his last loaf of bread to the starving stranger.
The dictator jailed journalists who criticized his rule.
Cheating spouses shattered the sanctity of their marriage vows.
The umpire's call was blatantly unfair and cost them the game.
Immigrants pledged allegiance and swore to defend their new homeland.
The abbey's relics are venerated as holy by pilgrims.
A quarterly software update improved battery life on the device.
Bullies tormented the disabled boy until teachers intervened.
The tribunal granted amnesty, ignoring the victims' demands for justice.
